msgs.text:
Turn 65 — certificate appendix frozen as a dependency graph I used only the files attached to this message. The target remains the exact over-G question: whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS. The attached prompt states this equivalently as the existence of an isomorphism commuting with the two canonical projections to G.The useful next step is not another Magnus layer or another special coefficient case. Turn 64 already froze the last notation issue: relation rows are right Λ=Z2 [[G]]-rows, and the final appendix must specify matrices by their basis action so that U0 and the coefficient shear N are not silently transposed. With that convention, the proof conclusion remains ΓS≅GΓH.

1. Fixed theorem statement

Use the fixed orientationρS:=rS−1.

This is harmless because rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure. The proof target is therefore the stronger statement:∃Φ:F⟶Fcontinuous over GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.Then Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,so Φ descends toΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F=ΓH.This is the conclusion already recorded in the attached sweep.

2. Basis-action convention

Let e0,e1 be the ordered basis corresponding to x0,x1. For a right relation row w=(a,b), define a displayed matrixU=(u00u01u10u11)by the basis actionU(e0)=e0u00+e1u10,U(e1)=e0u01+e1u11.Then (a,b)U=(au00+bu10,au01+bu11).

Under this convention, the dangerous Ef-piece H1 -matrix isU0=( s−11 s−22),meaning (s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),det(U0)=s.

The coefficient-normalizing shear isN=(10−(s+1)1),meaning(s2+1,s−1)N=(2,t),t=s−1.This basis-action convention is the whole point of Turn 64: the matrices are correct, but the appendix must prevent a transpose ambiguity.

3. Final certificate dependency graph

The final proof should be presented as a certificate theorem:

Certificate 0 — orientation

PrintρS=rS−1.

All finite and profinite substitutions send ρS to rH.

Certificate A — H1 -Nielsen matrix

Verify, over Λ=Z2 [[G]], that the linearized rows arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),and that a global U∈GL2 (Λ) satisfieswSU=wH.

On the Ef-piece, this includes the displayed U0 above. The other idempotent pieces are unit-controlled or swaps, so the first Magnus layer is invertible.

Certificate B — primitive-boundary verifier

Use the reduced-coproduct construction to computeLn=PrimnF2⟨A,B⟩and the boundary maps∂d:Ld⊕Ld→Ld+1,(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v].The attached history records that the executable linear verifier reproduceddimL1,…,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105 and the corresponding boundary and secondary quotient ranks.

Certificate C — residual restricted-Lie induction

On the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 piece, the equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q.The first nonzero error is primitive. The obstruction quotient satisfiesLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even.

The sweep provesCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2]. Thus the residual tower closes to all orders.

Certificate D — nonlinear finite blocks

Keep the four finite witness blocks:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4 content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggled=8 quotient witness certificate six independent Bockstein shadowsY0↦Y0Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rankD5,6,7=60, rank(μ4B4D5,6,7)=61θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2], λ((B8)[2])=1.The D.3 rank jump is the last executable finite certificate: since H4(2)is one-dimensional, the jump 60→61 proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to μ4=0.

Certificate E — coefficient Koszul–Witt induction

On the Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,the raw local row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t).After normalization, the coefficient problem is governed by the regular Koszul row (α,β) ingr(2,t)

R≅F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t).The first t-layer is contractible; the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4);and higher (2,t)-adic layers are handled by the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade, ordered by Magnus root degree first. The sweep emphasizes that actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a single naive Frobenius term.

Certificate F — finite filtered Nielsen compactness

For each degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, setMN,J=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩/(IN+JM),PN,J=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Define SN,J to be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 with first layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for τσ=τ2, and sendingρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J.

The certificates above implySN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. The sets are finite and functorial under refinement, so compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point. This yields a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→FwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.The finite filtered Nielsen compactness step is explicitly used to avoid both the false degree-only finite quotient and any Hopficity shortcut for the infinite pro-2 kernel.

Turn 65 conclusion The proof should now be frozen as a certificate theorem. No new obstruction layer should be opened. The row-action convention from Turn 64 eliminates the last transpose ambiguity, and Certificates 0–F form the dependency graph whose outputs implyΓS≅GΓH.The only remaining task is external reproducibility: attach the verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots for the finite certificate blocks, especially the D.3 rank jump 60→61. The attached sweep already records that no structural mathematical gap remains inside the proof.

ESTABLISHED: The final proof is frozen as a certificate contract with fixed orientation ρS=rS−1, basis-action matrix convention, H1 -Nielsen start, primitive-boundary verifier, residual induction, nonlinear certificates D.1–D.4, normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness. These produce a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining gap is external reproducibility/presentation: the standalone appendix should include verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots for the finite certificate outputs, especially D.3’s stack-rank 60→61.

NEXT: Write the final manuscript appendix exactly as Certificates 0–F, with the basis-action convention printed before U0 and N, and with verifier outputs attached rather than reopening any further Magnus or coefficient subcase.[TURN65-CERTIFICATE-CONTRACT-FROZEN-ISO]
